UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the application, linked to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically corresponds to the user's ID from the authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth UID).

username.description: The user's chosen display name, which must be unique.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture, stored in a cloud storage service.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

RustServer.title: RustServer
RustServer.description: Represents a single Rust game server, providing details for display in the server directory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RustServer entity.

name.description: The human-readable name of the Rust server.

ipAddress.description: The IP address or hostname of the Rust server.

port.description: The port number used to connect to the Rust server.

region.description: The geographical region where the server is hosted (e.g., 'NA East', 'EU West').

gameMode.description: The primary game mode or type of server (e.g., 'Vanilla', 'Modded', 'PVP', 'PVE').

currentPopulation.description: The current number of players online on the server.

maxPopulation.description: The maximum number of players the server can host.

status.description: The current operational status of the server (e.g., 'Online', 'Offline', 'Restarting').

description.description: A brief description or ruleset for the server.

websiteUrl.description: An optional URL to the server's official website or community page.

lastCheckedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the server's status and population were last updated.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the server entry was first added to the directory.

Topic.title: Topic
Topic.description: Represents a discussion topic or thread on the community message board.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Topic entity.

title.description: The title of the discussion topic.

content.description: The initial post or main content of the topic.

authorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this topic. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Topic)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the topic was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the topic's content was updated, or a reply was added.

lastReplyAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the last reply was made to this topic.

replyCount.description: The total number of replies (posts) associated with this topic (denormalized for quick display).

viewCount.description: The number of times this topic has been viewed.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents a single message or reply within a community message board topic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

topicId.description: Reference to the Topic this post belongs to. (Relationship: Topic 1:N Post)

authorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this post. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Post)

content.description: The content of the post or reply.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the post was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the post was updated.

NewsArticle.title: NewsArticle
NewsArticle.description: Represents a news article or update curated for the Rust365 news hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NewsArticle entity.

title.description: The title of the news article.

summary.description: A short summary or snippet of the article's content.

content.description: The full body content of the news article.

imageUrl.description: URL to a featured image for the news article.

sourceName.description: The name of the original publication or source of the news.

sourceUrl.description: URL to the original news article on the source website.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the news article was originally published by its source.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the news article was added to Rust365.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the news article entry was updated in Rust365.

ContentCreator.title: ContentCreator
ContentCreator.description: Represents a prominent Rust content creator showcased in the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContentCreator entity.

name.description: The display name or handle of the content creator.

platform.description: The primary platform where the creator publishes content (e.g., 'YouTube', 'Twitch').

profileUrl.description: URL to the creator's main profile page on their platform.

latestContentUrl.description: URL to the creator's latest video, stream, or guide.

latestContentTitle.description: The title of the creator's latest piece of content.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the creator's profile picture or avatar.

description.description: A brief description of the content creator or their content style.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the content creator entry was added to Rust365.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the content creator entry was updated in Rust365.

AppConfiguration.title: AppConfiguration
AppConfiguration.description: Stores global application configuration settings, including URLs for AI-generated visual assets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AppConfiguration entity. Expected to be a singleton (e.g., 'global').

heroImageUrl.description: URL to the main AI-generated hero background image for the application.

brandLogoUrl.description: URL to the main AI-generated brand logo for the application.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp indicating when the application configuration was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data, identified by the user's authentication UID. Write access is restricted to the user owning the profile (request.auth.uid == userId). Other authenticated users can read public profile fields.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Collection of Rust game servers. Publicly readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrative roles or backend services to maintain data integrity and curation.

params.name: serverId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Rust game server.

definition.description: Main discussion topics on the community message board. Publicly readable by all users. Creation requires authentication. Editing/deletion is restricted to the `authorId` or administrative roles. Includes denormalized `replyCount` and `lastReplyAt` for display, and `authorId` for authorization independence.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique identifier for a message board topic.

definition.description: Individual replies or messages within a specific message board topic. Publicly readable by all users. Creation requires authentication. Editing/deletion is restricted to the `authorId` or administrative roles. Includes `authorId` for authorization independence.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent topic.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier for a post within a topic.

definition.description: Curated news articles and updates for the Rust community. Publicly readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrative roles or backend services for content management.

params.name: articleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a news article.

definition.description: Featured Rust content creators. Publicly readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrative roles or backend services for curation.

params.name: creatorId
params.description: The unique identifier for a content creator.

definition.description: Global application configuration settings (e.g., AI-generated visual asset URLs). Expected to contain a single document (e.g., ID 'global'). Publicly readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrative roles or backend services.

params.name: configId
params.description: The unique identifier for an application configuration document (e.g., 'global').

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for Rust365 prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering strictly to the core design principles. The primary strategy for Authorization Independence is achieved through explicit denormalization of ownership fields and structural segregation of data with different security postures.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User-Owned Data (`/userProfiles/{userId}`, `/topics/{topicId}`, `/topics/{topicId}/posts/{postId}`):** Each document that requires specific user-based write authorization (e.g., editing one's own profile, topic, or post) explicitly contains an `authorId` or the document ID itself (`userId`) matches `request.auth.uid`. For example, a `Post` document includes `authorId`, allowing security rules to verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.authorId` without needing to `get()` the parent `Topic` document. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic significantly. All timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `lastReplyAt`) are stored directly on the relevant documents, maintaining invariants without cross-document lookups for authorization.
2.  **Global, Public Data (`/rustServers`, `/newsArticles`, `/contentCreators`, `/appConfiguration`):** These collections hold data that is globally accessible for reading and does not depend on specific user authentication or context for read access. Write access to these collections is implicitly designated for backend processes or administrative roles, not directly by end-users. This inherent lack of user-specific read authorization dependencies naturally ensures authorization independence.

**Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs) / Rules are not Filters:**
1.  **Structural Segregation for QAPs:** Different collections are used for data with distinct access requirements. For instance, `/userProfiles` allows users to write to their own profile but read others (or restricted fields). `/rustServers`, `/newsArticles`, `/contentCreators`, and `/appConfiguration` are entirely public-read. `/topics` and `/topics/{topicId}/posts` are public-read for all, while write access is restricted to authenticated users (or the original author for edits).
2.  **Homogeneous Security Posture:** Each collection (`/rustServers`, `/newsArticles`, `/contentCreators`, `/topics`, `/userProfiles`) and subcollection (`/topics/{topicId}/posts`) maintains a consistent security posture across all its documents. This means rules can be applied uniformly to the entire collection, preventing a `list` query from returning a subset of documents based on complex, in-rule authorization logic, which would effectively turn rules into filters. Instead, a `list` query on `/topics` will return all available topics, and individual `read` rules will be applied per document, with write rules enforced separately for authenticated users or authors. For public-read collections, `list` queries will simply retrieve all documents as allowed.

By following these principles, the Firestore structure promotes clear authorization intent, simplifies security rules (eliminating `get()` calls), and supports efficient and secure `list` queries, leading to a robust and maintainable backend.
